mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding one percentage point signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions and consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 1.0pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive SPX returns, with a negative or null Rank IC over a 2-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns during expansion regimes, or the predictive coefficient turns negative when filtered for non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across a 5-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index year-over-year growth surpassing three percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC during high-inflation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising nominal GDP indicates supply bottlenecks and cost-push inflation pressures, which erode corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative divergence between INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict negative SPX returns, with a positive or null Rank IC over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real personal consumption expenditures signal strong consumer spending momentum, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during low-interest-rate environments.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the predictive relationship reverses during rate-hiking cycles.
